chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Abdul Hamid Bhurgri Constitution Petition No. D-67 of 2020 [Suhabat Ali Awan Versus Province of Sindh and others] Petitioner: Mr. Muhammad Ali Pirzada, Advocate holds brief on behalf of Mr. Shakeel Ahmed G. Ansari, Advocate for the Petitioner. Respondents: Through Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, Addl. Advocate General, Sindh. Mr. Abdul Qadir Abro, Advocate Date of Hearing: 10-03-2026 Date of Order: 10-03-2026 O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. – Mr. Muhammad Ali Pirzada Advocate holding brief for Mr. Shakeel Ahmed Ansari, Advocate for Petitioner, requests for an adjournment. However, given the comments of the Respondents discussed infra, we are not inclined to adjourn this petition which has been pending for quite some time. 2. The Petitioner prays for resumption of salary and regularization of his service. The Petitioner submits that he was appointed by the Municipal Committee Shikarpur as drainage khalasi on daily wages; that having served the municipality for 5 to 6 years, he moved an application for regularization of service, which was not considered; instead, his salary was stopped; therefore, he filed petition apprehending termination of employment. 3. On the other hand, comments of the Municipal Committee Shikarpur are that the Petitioner was terminated from service in 2 September 2018 because he remained absent from work, and when he did attend, he was usually intoxicated. 4. Apparently, the Petitioner suppressed the fact that he was terminated from service due to misconduct two years before he filed the petition.

On the other hand, comments of the Municipal Committee Shikarpur are that the Petitioner was terminated from service in 2 September 2018 because he remained absent from work, and when he did attend, he was usually intoxicated. 4. Apparently, the Petitioner suppressed the fact that he was terminated from service due to misconduct two years before he filed the petition. Since he has not challenged such termination, the prayer for salary or regularization of service is out of the question. Resultantly, the petition is dismissed as misconceived. JUDGE JUDGE Zulfiqar
